label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Wait, February's last day is usually the 28th, except in leap years when it's the 29th. But 2001 isn't a leap year, right? Leap years are every 4 years, like 2000, 2004, 2008, etc. So 2001's February had 28 days. So Jane's birthday is February 28, 2001. Today is her 16th birthday. That means today's date must be February 28, 2017. Because 2001 plus 16 years is 2017. But wait, hold on. Let me check if 2017 is a leap year. No, 2017 isn't a leap year. So the last day of February would still be the 28th. So her 16th birthday is on 02/28/2017. Now, the question asks for the date one year ago from today. So if today is 02/28/2017, then one year ago would be 02/28/2016. But wait, 2016 is a leap year. Does that affect anything? Well, if you go back one year from 02/28/2017, you just subtract one year, which would be 02/28/2016. Even though 2016 has a 29th of February, since the original
